summary: Backend production endpoint validation executed against redirected production URL; all required API checks failed with 404 due to deployment/routing mismatch.

critical.issue: Expected 200 with ok:true, got 404 HTML page on redirected production host.
Expected usage JSON payload, got 404 HTML page.
Expected JSON object from Redis, got 404 HTML page.
Persistence flow blocked because endpoint not found (404).
Expected 405 for unsupported method, but endpoint returned 404.

action_items: Point production/custom domain to deployment that includes /api/health, /api/stats, /api/results serverless handlers.
Verify Vercel project/domain mapping (current domain redirects to trackwc2026.vercel.app where handlers are missing).
After domain/deployment fix, re-run endpoint contract tests to confirm Redis and STATS integrations are live.
critical_code_review_comments: Handlers exist in repository (/app/wc2026tracker/api/*.js), but production URL currently resolves to an app/deployment returning Next.js 404 for these routes.
No endpoint-level business logic could be validated because routing fails before handler execution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL was not available in environment. Used redirected host discovered from https://wc2026tracker.vercel.app -> https://trackwc2026.vercel.app. All required /api routes returned 404; re-test only after Vercel domain/deployment mapping is corrected.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: call https://wc2026tracker.vercel.app/api/health -> 307 redirect to https://trackwc2026.vercel.app/api/health; redirected URL returns 404 HTML (not handler JSON). Same for /api/stats and /api/results. Root cause: production domain points to deployment/application that does not expose expected serverless API handlers. Mitigation: fix Vercel project/domain linkage and redeploy commit containing /api/health.js, /api/stats.js, /api/results.js with required env vars configured.
